name: sctefu

metadata.name: sctefu
metadata.summary: Fumatic SCTE-35 Encoder/ Decoder for MPEGTS, HLS , DASH and everything else...

documentation:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/adae03a1-9f85-403e-9dce-d134e0996903)



> I wrote threefive because I couldn't find any SCTE-35 tools. There were a few libraries, but SCTE-35 wasn't their focus,
> and they required writing several hundred lines of code to do anything. I think a library should save you time.
# Why sctefu, why not just threefive?
* __Answer #1__  <s>Github F2A'ed out of my account, and I have no idea where I put the recovery codes. I have more than one Internet account, where I am supposed to keep all this data? Should I store in some company's cloud so they can read it or give it away to Eastern European script kiddies? F2A is complete nonsense. It doesn't matter how secure it is, if a security measure prevents me from accessing my account, that's not secure, that's locked out.</s>
* __Answer #2__     <s>Two repos with the same name, would be confusing.</s>
* __Answer #3__  <s>I wanted to rethink everything. After going through the code, I kept about 75% of it. I completely rewrote the Xml parser,I did a lot of work with the Cue class, HLS, and really tuned up the cli. The cli is amazing and super easy to use.</s>
*  __Answer #4__  I came up with a really cool name, but it wasn't available on pypi.org for a package name.
*  __Answer #5__  <s>Really, I was just getting a little bored with the whole thing, I just wanted to shake things up a little, keep it fun.</s>
*  __Answer #6__ <s>Have you seen chewy Tic Tacs? They're just mini jelly beans.</s>
*  __Answer #7__ <s>I'll tell you the truth, everything is a lie.</s>
*  __Answer #8__ <s>Obama is a lizard. I'm not sure about his wife.</s>

# How do I upgrade my code to sctefu?
> Despite making a lot of changes, the api remains. sctefu is pretty much a drop in replacement for threefive. I used sed to upgrade my code.
```js
sed -e 's/threefive/sctefu/g'
```
> One thing to note, calling load or decode is no longer necessary for the Cue class, however, the methods are there in case you do call them.
>
> 
# Is sctefu faster than threefive?
###  `threefive` vs. `sctefu`  vs. `sctefu next release`

# Now with Super Advanced Error Detection and Stuff. 
* This is super cool. 
* Does not generate Fatal errors, it won't break your process.
* Displays what is in error and how to correct it.
* Works in the cli
* Works in code.
  ![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/50331e73-cd0a-46d6-b265-1b212d625737)


# Issues and Bugs.
* If you think you have a bug, I'll quickly fix it, but first I need you to prove it to me. Show me the entire error message, the code you're running, and the SCTE-35.
---
# Special Requests 
* If need some work done, this is what I do for a living, you can hire me.
* If you want to discuss your project open an issue and I'll send you my contact info.
---

# `Install`
* python3 via pip
```rebol
python3 -mpip install sctefu
```
* pypy3 
```rebol
pypy3 -mpip install sctefu
```
* from the git repo
```rebol
git clone https://github.com/superkabuki/scte35.git
cd scte35
make install
```
___

# `The Cli tool`
> The cli audetects data being available on stdin and that allows it to autodetect the SCTE-35 format as well as MPEGTS steams. Now every SCTE-35 data format, except hls, is autodetected on the commandline, as well as stdin.HLS requires the `hls` ketyword.

### The cli tool installs automatically with pip or the Makefile.


* [__SCTE-35 Inputs__](#inputs)
* [__SCTE-35 Outputs__](#outputs)
* [Parse __MPEGTS__ streams for __SCTE-35__](#streams)
* [Parse __SCTE-35__ in __hls__](#hls)
* [Display __MPEGTS__ __iframes__](#iframes)
* [Display raw __SCTE-35 packets__ from __video streams__](#packets)
* [__Repair SCTE-35 streams__ changed to __bin data__ by __ffmpeg__](#sixfix)


# `Inputs`

* Most __inputs__ are __auto-detected.__ 
* __stdin__ is __auto selected__ and __auto detected.__
* __SCTE-35 data is printed to stderr__
* __stdout is used when piping video__

| Input Type |     Cli Example                                                                                             |
|------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| __Base64__     |  `sctefu '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU='`
| __Hex__        |`sctefu 0xfc301600000000000000fff00506fed605225b0000b0b65f3b`|
| __HLS__         |`sctefu hls https://example.com/master.m3u8`                                                             |
| __JSON__        |`sctefu < json.json`  |
| __Xml__         | `sctefu  < xml.xml`                                                                                     |
| __Xmlbin__      | `sctefu < xmlbin.xml`                                                                                   |

# Streams

|Protocol       |  Cli Example                                                                                                                                       |
|---------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|  File         |   `sctefu video.ts`                                                                                                                            |
|  Http(s)      |   `sctefu https://example.com/video.ts`                                                                                                        |
|  Stdin        |  `sctefu < video.ts`            |
|  UDP Multicast|  `sctefu udp://@235.35.3.5:9999`                                                                          |
|  UDP Unicast  |                                                                      `sctefu udp://10.0.0.7:5555`                                              |
|  HLS          |                                                                                                    `sctefu hls https://example.com/master.m3u8`|
|               |                                                                                                                                                    |


### Outputs
* output type is determined by the key words __base64, bytes, hex, int, json, xml, and xmlbin__.
* __json is the default__.
* __Any input (except HLS,) can be returned as any output__
  * examples __Base64 to Hex__, or  __Mpegts to Xml__, etc...) 


| Output Type | Cli Example         |
|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
|__Base 64__     |                                                                                                                                                                    `sctefu 0xfc301600000000000000fff00506fed605225b0000b0b65f3b  base64  `                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| __Bytes__       |                                                                                 `sctefu 0xfc301600000000000000fff00506fed605225b0000b0b65f3b  bytes`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Hex         | `sctefu '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU='  hex`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Integer     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       `sctefu '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU='  int`   |
| JSON        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              `sctefu 0xfc301600000000000000fff00506fed605225b0000b0b65f3b json ` |
| Xml         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        `sctefu '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU=' xml `                                                                                 `         |
| Xml+bin     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        `sctefu 0xfc301600000000000000fff00506fed605225b0000b0b65f3b xmlbin   `      |`

### `hls`
* parse hls manifests and segments for SCTE-35
```rebol
sctefu hls https://example.com/master.m3u8
```
___
### `Iframes`
* Show iframes PTS in an MPEGTS video
```rebol
sctefu iframes https://example.com/video.ts
```
___
### `packets`   
* Print raw SCTE-35 packets from multicast mpegts video
```rebol
sctefu packets udp://@235.35.3.5:3535
```
___
### `proxy`   
* Parse a https stream and write raw video to stdout
```rebol
sctefu proxy video.ts
```
___
### `pts`    
* Print PTS from mpegts video
```rebol
 sctefu pts video.ts
```
___
### `sidecar`  
* Parse a stream, write pts,write SCTE-35 Cues to sidecar.txt
```rebol
  sctefu sidecar video.ts
```
___
### `sixfix`  
* Fix SCTE-35 data mangled by ffmpeg
```rebol
 sctefu sixfix video.ts
```
___
### `show`  

* Probe mpegts video _( kind of like ffprobe )_
```rebol
 sctefu show video.ts
```
___
### `version`     
* Show version
```rebol
 sctefu version
```
___
### `help`        
* Help
```rebol
 sctefu help
```

new_reader.py
Home of the reader function

functions.name: sctefu.new_reader.reader
documentation:
reader returns an open file handle.
stdin:              cat video.ts | gumd
files:              "/home/you/video.ts"
http(s) urls:       "https://example.com/vid.ts"
 (http headers can be added by setting headers)
udp urls:           "udp://1.2.3.4:5555"
multicast urls:     "udp://@227.1.3.10:4310"

Use like:

with reader('http://iodisco.com/') as disco:
    disco.read()

with reader('http://iodisco.com/',headers={"myHeader":"DOOM"}) as doom:
    doom.read()

with reader("udp://@227.1.3.10:4310") as data:
    data.read(8192)

with reader("/home/you/video.ts") as data:
    fu = data.read()

udp_data =reader("udp://1.2.3.4:5555")
chunks = [udp_data.read(188) for i in range(0,1024)]
udp_data.close()

methods.name: sctefu.segment.Segment.decode
documentation: decode a mpegts segment.

The Segment class is a Sub Class of sctefu.Stream
made for small, fixed size MPEGTS files,like HLS segments.

Segment Class Specific Features:

* Decryption of AES Encrypted MPEGTS.

* Segment.cues  a list of SCTE35 cues found in the segment.


Example:

    from sctefu import Segment

    >>>> uri = "https://example.com/1.ts"
    >>>> seg = Segment(uri)
    >>>> seg.decode()
    >>>> [cue.encode() for cue in seg.cues]
    ['/DARAAAAAAAAAP/wAAAAAHpPv/8=',
    '/DAvAAAAAAAAAP/wFAUAAAKWf+//4WoauH4BTFYgAAEAAAAKAAhDVUVJAAAAAOv1oqc=']


AES Encryption Example:

    from sctefu import Segment

    >>>> key = "https://example.com/aes.key"
    >>>> IV=0x998C575D24F514AEC84EDC5CABCCDB81
    >>>> uri = "https://example.com/aes-1.ts"

    >>>> seg = Segment(uri,key_uri=key, iv=IV)
    >>>> seg.decode()
    >>>> {cue.packet_data.pts:cue.encode() for cue in seg.cues}

   { 89718.451333: '/DARAAAAAAAAAP/wAAAAAHpPv/8=',
   89730.281789: '/DAvAAAAAAAAAP/wFAUAAAKWf+//4WoauH4BTFYgAAEAAAAKAAhDVUVJAAAAAOv1oqc='}

methods.name: sctefu.xml.Node.add_comment
documentation: add_comment add a Comment node

The Node class is to create an xml node.

An instance of Node has:

    name :      <name> </name>
    value  :    <name>value</name>
    attrs :     <name attrs[k]="attrs[v]">
    children :  <name><children[0]></children[0]</name>
    depth:      tab depth for printing (automatically set)

Use like this:

    from sctefu.xml import Node

    ts = Node('TimeSignal')
    st = Node('SpliceTime',attrs={'pts_time':3442857000})
    ts.add_child(st)
    print(ts)

classes.name: sctefu.xml.Comment
documentation:
The Comment class is to create a Node representing a xml comment.

An instance of Comment has:

    name :      <!-- name -->
    depth:      tab depth for printing (automatically set)

Since Comment is a Node, it also has attrs, value and children but
these are ignored. cf etree.Comment
Use like this:

    from sctefu.xml import Comment, Node

    n = Node('root')
    c = Comment('my first comment')

    n.add_child(c)
    print(n)

See also Node.add_comment:

methods.name: sctefu.cue.Cue._no_cmd
documentation: _no_cmd raises an exception if no splice command.

methods.name: sctefu.cue.Cue.load
documentation:
Cue.load loads SCTE35 data for encoding.
gonzo is a dict or json
with any or all of these keys
gonzo = {
    'info_section': {dict} ,
    'command': {dict},
    'descriptors': [list of {dicts}],
    }

* load doesn't need to be called directly
  unless you initialize a Cue without data.

methods.name: sctefu.cue.Cue._from_xml
documentation:
_from_xml converts xml to data that can
be loaded by a Cue instance.

methods.name: sctefu.cue.Cue._xml_mk_descriptor
documentation: _mk_descriptor_xml make xml nodes for descriptors.

methods.name: sctefu.cue.Cue.xml
documentation:
xml returns a sctefu.Node instance
which can be edited as needed or printed.
xmlbin

methods.name: sctefu.cue.Cue.xmlbin
documentation:
xml returns a sctefu.Node instance
which can be edited as needed or printed.
xmlbin

The sctefu.Cue class handles parsing
SCTE 35 message strings.
Example usage:

>>>> import sctefu
>>>> Base64 = "/DAvAAAAAAAA///wBQb+dGKQoAAZAhdDVUVJSAAAjn+fCAgAAAAALKChijUCAKnMZ1g="
>>>> cue = sctefu.Cue(Base64)
>>>> cue.show()

* A cue instance can be initialized with
 Base64, Bytes, Hex, Int, Json, Xml, or Xml+binary data.

* Instance variables can be accessed via dot notation.

>>>> cue.command
{'command_length': 5, 'name': 'Time Signal', 'time_specified_flag': True,
'pts_time': 21695.740089}

>>>> cue.command.pts_time
21695.740089

documentation: fu slice up exemel into data chunks.

modules.name: sctefu.stream
documentation: Mpeg-TS Stream parsing class Stream

functions.name: sctefu.stream.no_op
documentation:
no_op is just a dummy func to pass to Stream.decode()
to suppress output.

functions.name: sctefu.stream.show_cue
documentation:
default function call for Stream.decode
when a SCTE-35 packet is found.

functions.name: sctefu.stream.show_cue_stderr
documentation:
print2 cue data to sys.stderr
for Stream.decode_proxy

classes.name: sctefu.stream.ProgramInfo

methods.name: sctefu.stream.ProgramInfo.show
documentation:
show print2 the Program Infomation
in a familiar format.

classes.name: sctefu.stream.Pids
documentation: Pids holds sets of pids for pat,pcr,pmt, and scte35

classes.name: sctefu.stream.Maps
documentation:
Maps holds mappings
pids mapped to continuity_counters,
programs, partial tables and last payload.

programs mapped to pcr and pts

classes.name: sctefu.stream.Stream

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.__init__
documentation:
tsdata is an file or http/https url
set show_null=False to exclude Splice Nulls

Use like...

from sctefu import Stream
strm = Stream("vid.ts",show_null=False)
strm.decode()

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.as_90k
documentation: as_90k returns ticks as 90k clock time

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_length
documentation: parse a 12 bit length value

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_pid
documentation: parse a 13 bit pid value

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_program
documentation: parse a 16 bit program number value

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._find_start
documentation:
_find_start locates the first SYNC_BYTE
parses 1 packet and returns True
if SYNC_BYTE is found.

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.iter_pkts
documentation: iter_pkts iterates a mpegts stream into packets

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.decode
documentation:
Stream.decode reads self.tsdata to find SCTE35 packets.
func can be set to a custom function that accepts
a sctefu.Cue instance as it's only argument.

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.decode_next
documentation:
Stream.decode_next returns the next
SCTE35 cue as a sctefu.Cue instance.

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.decode_program
documentation:
Stream.decode_program limits SCTE35 parsing
to a specific MPEGTS program.

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.decode_pids
documentation:
Stream.decode_pids takes a list of SCTE-35 Pids parse
and an optional call back function to run when a Cue is found.
if scte35_pids is not set, all sctefu pids will be parsed.

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.decode_start_time
documentation: decode_start_time

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.proxy
documentation:
Stream.decode_proxy writes all ts packets are written to stdout
for piping into another program like mplayer.
SCTE-35 cues are print2`ed to stderr.

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.show
documentation:
displays streams that will be
parsed for SCTE-35.

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.show_pts
documentation: show_pts displays current pts by pid.

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.pts
documentation: pts returns a dict of  program:pts

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.pid2prgm
documentation:
pid2prgm takes a pid,
returns the program

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.pid2pts
documentation:
pid2pts takes a pid
returns the current pts

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream.pid2pcr
documentation:
pid2pcr takes a pid
returns the current pcr

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_pts
documentation:
parse pts and store by program key
in the dict Stream._pid_pts

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_payload
documentation: _parse_payload returns the packet payload

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_tables
documentation:
_parse_tables parse for
PAT, PMT,  and SDT tables
based on pid of the pkt

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_info
documentation:
_parse_info parses the packet for tables
and returns the pid

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_scte35
documentation: parse a sctefu cue from one or more packets

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_sdt
documentation: _parse_sdt parses the SDT for program metadata

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_pat
documentation:
parse program association table
for program to pmt_pid mappings.

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_pmt
documentation: parse program maps for streams

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_program_streams
documentation:
parse the elementary streams
from a program

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._parse_stream_type
documentation: extract stream pid and type

methods.name: sctefu.stream.Stream._set_scte35_pids
documentation:
if stream_type is 0x06 or 0x86
add it to self._scte35_pids.
Stream class for parsing MPEG-TS data.

modules.name: sctefu.bitn
documentation: The bitn.BitBin and bitn.NBin classes

classes.name: sctefu.bitn.BitBin

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.BitBin.as_90k
documentation:
Returns num_bits
of bits as 90k time

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.BitBin.as_int
documentation:
Starting at self.idx of self.bits,
slice off num_bits of bits.

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.BitBin.as_hex
documentation:
Returns the hex value
of num_bits of bits

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.BitBin.as_charset
documentation:
Returns num_bits of bits
as bytes decoded as charset
default charset is ascii.

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.BitBin.as_bytes
documentation:
Returns num_bits of bits
as bytes

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.BitBin.as_flag
documentation: Returns one bit as True or False

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.BitBin.forward
documentation:
Advances the start point
forward by num_bits

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.BitBin.negative_shift
documentation:
negative_shift is called instead of
throwing a negative shift count error.

bitn.Bitbin takes a byte string and
converts it to a integer, a very large integer
if needed. A 1500 bit integer is no problem.
several methods are available for slicing off bits.

classes.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin.nbits2bites
documentation:
nbits2bites converts
the int self.nbits to bytes as self.bites
and sets self.nbits  and self.idx to 0

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin.add_bites
documentation:
add_bites appends plus_bites
to self.bites

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin.add_int
documentation: left shift nbits and append new_bits
if self.idx % 8 == 0:
self.nbits2bites()

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin.add_90k
documentation:
Converts 90k  float timestamps
to an int and appends it to nbits
via self.add_int

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin.add_hex
documentation:
add_hex converts a
hex encoded string to an int
and appends it to self.nbits
via self.add_int

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin.add_flag
documentation:
add_flag takes a boolean
value and adds it as an integer
to self.nbits via self.add_int

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin.reserve
documentation:
reserve sets 'num'  bits to 1
and appends them to self.nbits
via self.add_int

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin.forward
documentation: Currently just an alias to reserve

methods.name: sctefu.bitn.NBin.zeroed
documentation: zeroed sets num bits to zero

bitn.NBin is
the reverse BitBin.
Encodes data to integers
and then bytes
